title: Microsoft cuts thousands of jobs in Xbox and restructures its development studios
summary: The company reduces its workforce by up to 4,800 positions and sells several studios in an attempt to improve margins in its video game business.

title: AMD launches its Ryzen AI Halo development PC for $3,999
summary: The system, limited to one retailer in the US, competes with Nvidia solutions and is aimed at AI developers.

title: Samsung's chip division annual profits surpass the last 40 years combined
summary: The South Korean manufacturer becomes the most profitable company in the world thanks to the AI memory boom.

title: Anthropic reveals that Claude has developed a hidden workspace
summary: The discovery of an internal 'J-space' reignites the debate on consciousness and transparency in advanced language models.

title: US, UK, and Scotland adopt opposing measures on data center regulation
summary: While the US relaxes pollution limits, the UK speeds up permits, and Scotland freezes projects amid the energy crisis.

title: The AI boom could generate a $7 trillion debt bubble, analysts warn
summary: AI profits outside the tech sector would take longer than Wall Street expects.

title: PlayStation faces criticism for its all-digital push while Xbox considers including a disc drive
summary: Sony's decision sparks a heated debate on social media, while Microsoft considers adding the optical drive in its next console.

title: Hidden backdoor discovered in cheap Tenda routers
summary: CERT/CC warns about a vulnerability that allows unauthorized remote access to the devices.

title: Sony headphones can be used as free head trackers for PC simulators
summary: An open-source app converts the headphone sensors into real-time camera controls for hundreds of games.

title: Steam Machine's 'red line of death' has an official cure: clear the CMOS
summary: The fault, previously thought to be a GPU problem, is easily resolved by erasing the system configuration memory.

title: Elon Musk's xAI merges with SpaceX and is renamed to SpaceXAI
summary: The unification of both companies seeks to simplify the corporate structure in the face of investor interest.

title: They manage to run Linux on the legendary 64-bit Atari Jaguar console
summary: The retro community succeeds in porting the operating system to a platform considered difficult to program and that failed commercially.

title: Windows device identifier allowed the FBI to track a hacker from the Scattered Spider group
summary: Microsoft can track users via the Device ID, a method used by authorities in a judicial investigation.

title: Nintendo discontinues the original Switch in Europe and aims for a Switch 2 with a removable battery
summary: The company ceases sales of the current console while rumors indicate the launch of an improved version for autumn.

title: A Meta data center contaminates a town's water with lethal bacteria
summary: The AI facility causes a health crisis by leaking dangerous microorganisms into the local supply.

title: US Supreme Court requires warrant to access phone location data
summary: The ruling strengthens digital privacy by requiring prior authorization to track users' location.

title: Illinois enacts one of the strictest artificial intelligence laws in the country
summary: Governor Pritzker signs bill 315, which imposes comprehensive regulations on AI development and use.

title: Netflix, pioneer of 'binge-watching', may be moving beyond the series marathon model
summary: The streaming platform explores new content strategies amid changing viewer habits.

title: Microsoft 365 Copilot adoption remains below 4.5% three years after launch
summary: Only 1% of users use it weekly, while the company increases service prices.

title: FCC eliminates rule that required internet providers to list all their fees
summary: The measure revokes a Biden-era regulation that aimed to bring transparency to costs for consumers.
